tool.name: WebAtlas Generate Image
tool.description: Generate label/raw TIFF image from spatial metadata
tool.command: #if $img_type.img_type_selector == "label": #if $img_type.input_type.input_type_selector == "visium": #if $img_type.input_type.file_type_visium.file_type_selector_visium == "zip": mkdir unpacked_spaceranger && unzip $input_visium_zip -d unpacked_spaceranger && generate_image.py --stem $stem --img_type label --file_type visium --file_path unpacked_spaceranger @VISIUM_LABEL_COMMAND@ mv $stem-label.tif $output_img #else if $img_type.input_type.file_type_visium.file_type_selector_visium == "h5ad": ln -s '$input_visium_h5ad' input.h5ad && generate_image.py --stem $stem --img_type label --file_type visium --file_path input.h5ad @VISIUM_LABEL_COMMAND@ mv $stem-label.tif $output_img #end if #else if $img_type.input_type.input_type_selector == "xenium": mkdir unpacked_xenium && unzip $input_xenium -d unpacked_xenium && generate_image.py --stem $stem --img_type label --file_type xenium --file_path unpacked_xenium --ref_img $ref_img --args "{ #if $resolution: 'resolution': $resolution #end if }" && mv $stem-label.tif $output_img #else if $img_type.input_type.input_type_selector == "merscope": mkdir unpacked_merscope && unzip $input_merscope_label -d unpacked_merscope && generate_image.py --stem $stem --img_type label --file_type merscope --file_path unpacked_merscope --ref_img $ref_img @Z_INDEX_COMMAND@ mv $stem-label.tif $output_img #end if #else if $img_type.img_type_selector == "raw": mkdir unpacked_merscope && unzip $input_merscope_raw -d unpacked_merscope && generate_image.py --stem $stem --img_type raw --file_type merscope --file_path unpacked_merscope @Z_INDEX_COMMAND@ mv $stem-raw.tif $output_img #end if

param.name: input_type_selector
param.label: Choose the type of input data
param.help: Choose the format of the input dataset to generate a label image from.
param.option: Visium data (SpaceRanger output or H5AD AnnData file)

param.name: file_type_selector_visium
param.label: Choose the type of input file

param.name: ref_img_visium
param.label: Reference image
param.help: Reference image from which to get the shape for the label image. If left empty the shape will be derived from the AnnData.

param.label: Sample ID
param.help: Specify a sample ID if the dataset contains multiple samples and you wish to visualize a specific one.

param.label: Relative size
param.help: Optional numerical 'obs' column name that holds a multiplier for the spot diameter. Only useful for data that has been processed to merge spots.

param.name: resolution
param.label: Pixel resolution
param.help: Pixel resolution used to convert Xenium spatial coordinates. Default value: 0.2125

tool.help:
This tool generates a label TIFF image based on spatial metadata of the input data.
For MERSCOPE data, it can additionally concatenate raw images into a single multichannel TIFF image.
----
**Supported Input Types**
- **Visium AnnData (H5AD)** - **Visium SpaceRanger output archive (ZIP)** - **Xenium output archive (ZIP)** - **MERSCOPE output archive (ZIP)**
----
**Functionality**
Depending on the input type, label images are generated as follows:
- **Visium** If SpaceRanger is provided as input, it is first converted into an AnnData object. The observations are then reindexed to ensure numerical values, which are required for label image generation. A label image TIFF file is then generated, with spots based on the spatial coordinates of the AnnData object. Each spot is assigned a unique identifier, for example, a cell ID.
- **Xenium** A label image is generated with labels based on cell segmentation polygons from the Xenium output. These polygons are converted into pixel coordinates and applied to the output image, assigning a unique label (e.g. cell ID) to each cell.
- **MERSCOPE** A label image is generated based on boundary information stored in the MERSCOPE data. Optionally, multiple Z-indices of the MERSCOPE data can be processed.
Additionally, this tool can concatenate raw TIFF images from MERSCOPE into a single multichannel TIFF image per Z-index. This process is performed independently of the label image generation.
----
**Reference Image** A reference image is required to determine the dimensions of the output label image. For Visium data, this is optional, as the shape can be derived from the AnnData.
----
**Input type specific parameters**
- **Visium** - Subset AnnData on an obs column: Allows filtering of observations based on a specified column in the *obs* table. Only observations matching the specified values are kept in the dataset. - Sample ID: Specify a sample ID if the dataset contains multiple samples and you wish to visualize a specific one. - Relative size: Optional numerical *obs* column name that holds a multiplier for the spot diameter. Only useful for data that has been processed to merge spots.
- **Xenium** - Pixel resolution: Pixel resolution used for converting Xenium spatial coordinates to pixel coordinates.
- **MERSCOPE** - Z index: Z index of the MERSCOPE data that should be processed.
----
**Output**
- **Label image (TIFF)** - **Multichannel raw image (TIFF, MERSCOPE only)**
The output is used as input for the downstream WebAtlas tools **WebAtlas Consolidate Metadata** and **WebAtlas Write**.
